COMPOSE THIS BUILDING PRIMARILY FROM `procedural` parts — avoid named component shortcuts. Build the massing from boxes, cylinders, and cones. Use at least 3 procedural parts for unique sculptural form.
USE AT LEAST ONE `procedural` COMPOUND SHAPE (barrel_roof, apse, tower, buttress, shed_roof, lattice_screen, or balustrade) to give this building a form no other building in the city shares.
MAKE THIS BUILDING ASYMMETRIC — one side taller, different window pattern left vs right, or an attached wing/extension using procedural boxes at an offset.
ADD DISTINCTIVE SURFACE ARTICULATION — use procedural boxes as decorative bands, cornices, string courses, or rusticated quoins at corners. The facade should have visual rhythm and depth.
EMPHASIZE THE ROOFLINE — use an unusual roof form: hipped_roof, shed_roof (procedural), barrel_roof (procedural), or a combination. The skyline silhouette should be unique.
FOCUS ON THE GROUND LEVEL — design a distinctive entrance zone: recessed doorway, projecting porch, flanking elements, or a shop-front arcade. The street-level identity matters most.
COMBINE MULTIPLE VOLUMES — this building should read as 2-3 connected masses of different heights, not a single box. Use procedural blocks at different positions to create a stepped or L-shaped plan.
ADD VERTICAL ACCENT — use a procedural tower, staircase turret, chimney stack, or raised parapet section that breaks the roofline and gives this building a landmark quality.
